data.content:
LVAD
LVAD stands for left ventricular assist device, a mechanical pump supporting the left ventricle.<SEP>LVAD (Left Ventricular Assist Device) is a mechanical device used to support heart function, mentioned in the context of additional work for patients.<SEP>LVAD is a left ventricular assist device used for hemodynamic support in cardiogenic shock patients.

CPI
CPI stands for cardiac power index, a measure of cardiac function.<SEP>CPI stands for Cardiac Power Index, a measure used to estimate hemodynamic support offered by a device.

IABP
IABP stands for intra-aortic balloon pump, a medical device used for hemodynamic support in patients with cardiogenic shock.<SEP>IABP stands for intra-aortic balloon pump, a mechanical device used to support heart function.<SEP>IABP (Intra-aortic Balloon Pump) is a mechanical device used for hemodynamic support, compared against Impella in the clinical trial.<SEP>IABP (intra-aortic balloon pump) is a cardiac assist device used as a comparator to Impella in the clinical trial.<SEP>IABP is an intra-aortic balloon pump used for hemodynamic support in cardiogenic shock patients.<SEP>IABP (Intra-Aortic Balloon Pump) is a medical device used for cardiac support, compared against Impella in a survival probability study.

MODS
MODS stands for Multiple Organ Dysfunction Score, a scoring system for organ failure.<SEP>MODS (Multiple Organ Dysfunction Score) is a scoring system used to assess organ dysfunction in the study.<SEP>MODS stands for Multiple Organ Dysfunction Score, a scoring system used to evaluate organ dysfunction.

AMI
AMI stands for acute myocardial infarction, a medical condition involving heart attack.<SEP>AMI is a concept or entity referenced in the input text, possibly standing for Acute Myocardial Infarction or another medical term.<SEP>A medical entity referenced in the text, likely standing for Acute Myocardial Infarction or a similar condition.

MAP
MAP stands for mean arterial pressure, a measure of average blood pressure in arteries.

CS
CS stands for cardiogenic shock, a life-threatening condition where the heart fails to pump enough blood.

CI
CI stands for cardiac index, a hemodynamic parameter measuring cardiac output relative to body surface area.

SOFA
SOFA stands for Sepsis-related Organ Failure Assessment, a scoring system for organ dysfunction.<SEP>SOFA (Sequential Organ Failure Assessment) is a scoring system used to assess organ failure in the<SEP>SOFA stands for Sepsis-related Organ Failure Assessment, a scoring system used to evaluate organ failure.

LVEF
LVEF stands for left ventricular ejection fraction, a measure

Impella LP2.5
The Impella LP2.5 is a catheter-based, impeller-driven, axial-flow pump manufactured by Abiomed Europe GmbH, capable of providing up to 2.5 l/min of flow from the left ventricle to the ascending aorta and is implantable via a percutaneous approach.<SEP>Impella LP2.5 is a type of ventricular assist device used in a clinical trial for cardiogenic shock.

World Athletics Federation
The World Athletics Federation is the governing body overseeing the World Athletics Championship and record validations.<SEP>The World Athletics Federation is the governing body overseeing the World Athletics Championship and record validations.<SEP>The World Athletics Federation is the governing body overseeing the World Athletics Championship and record validations.

Acute Myocardial Infarction
Acute myocardial infarction is a medical event causing cardiogenic shock, studied in the clinical trial.

Impella
Impella is a catheter-based miniaturized rotary blood pump, specifically the Impella LP2.5 model, used for hemodynamic support in patients with cardiogenic shock.<SEP>Impella is a ventricular assist device used in patients with cardiogenic shock, associated with higher hemolysis and blood product administration compared to IABP.<SEP>Impella is a catheter-based, miniaturized rotary blood pump used as a new LVAD for percutaneous access in cardiogenic shock patients.<SEP>Impella is a medical device used as a treatment in a clinical trial, compared against intra-aortic balloon pump.<SEP>Impella is a medical device used for cardiac support, compared against IABP in a survival probability study.

Cardiogenic Shock
Cardiogenic shock is a medical condition characterized by the heart's inability to pump sufficient blood, often caused by acute myocardial infarction.

Serum Lactate
Serum lactate is a metabolic parameter measured in patients, with lower levels observed in the Impella group during the first 48 hours.<SEP>Serum lactate is a biomarker of tissue perfusion and shock, measured over time in Impella and IABP patients.<SEP>Serum lactate is a biomarker measured to assess tissue hypoxia and metabolic status in patients.

Randomized Clinical Trial
A randomized clinical trial is the study<SEP>A Randomized Clinical Trial is the study design comparing Impella and Intra-Aortic Balloon Pump treatments.

Survival Probability
Survival Probability is a statistical measure shown on the y-axis of the Kaplan-Meier curves, comparing outcomes between Impella and IABP groups.

Days After Randomization
Days After Randomization is the time variable on the x-axis of the survival curves, ranging from 0 to 30 days.

30 Days
30 Days is the follow-up time point at which MODS and SOFA scores were measured and survival was assessed.<SEP>30 Days is the endpoint time point in the survival analysis following randomization.

Log-Rank P=0.97
Log-Rank P=0.97 is the statistical test result indicating no significant difference in survival between the Impella and IABP groups.

Investor Concerns
Investor concerns refer to the worries of investors regarding rising interest rates and regulatory uncertainty, which contributed to the market selloff.<SEP>Investor concerns over rising interest rates and regulatory uncertainty are a key factor driving the market selloff.<SEP>Investor concerns over rising interest rates and regulatory uncertainty are a key factor driving the market selloff.<SEP>Investor concerns over rising interest rates and regulatory uncertainty are driving the market selloff.<SEP>Investor concerns over rising interest rates and regulatory uncertainty are a key factor driving the market selloff.

Baseline
Baseline is the initial time point at which MODS and SOFA scores were measured before treatment.<SEP>Baseline is the starting point (Day 0) in the survival analysis before treatment randomization.

Rising Interest Rates
Rising interest rates are a macroeconomic factor that has become a key concern for investors, contributing to widespread investor anxiety and market volatility. This phenomenon is identified as a primary driver behind the stock market downturn and the broader market selloff, as it directly influences investor sentiment and financial market stability.

Regulatory Uncertainty
Regulatory uncertainty refers to the lack of clarity or the presence of unclear and changing government policies and regulations that affect business operations. This ambiguity is a key factor driving investor concerns, as it makes it difficult to predict future compliance costs, operational constraints, and the overall business environment. Consequently, regulatory uncertainty is a significant contributor to market volatility, often leading to market downturns, selloffs, and declines in specific sectors such as technology stocks. The absence of stable and predictable rules creates an environment of risk that prompts cautious investor behavior and negatively impacts market stability.<SEP>Regulatory uncertainty refers to unclear or changing regulations that negatively impact investor sentiment and market performance.<SEP>Regulatory uncertainty is a factor contributing to investor concerns and the decline in tech stocks.

Federal Reserve
The Federal Reserve is the central banking system and central bank of the United States. Its policy decisions are closely watched by financial experts and are expected to influence both investor confidence and overall market stability. The institution’s upcoming policy announcement is a key event anticipated to have a significant impact on these areas.

Financial Experts
Financial experts are analysts and professionals who monitor market conditions and the actions of the Federal Reserve. Their primary focus is on closely watching the Federal Reserve's next move, particularly regarding potential rate hikes, and analyzing the subsequent impact on market stability and overall financial markets.

ISAR-SHOCK Trial
The ISAR-SHOCK trial is a prospective, 2-center, randomized clinical trial comparing the Impella LP2.5 to IABP in patients with cardiogenic shock caused by acute myocardial infarction.

Abiomed Europe GmbH
Abiomed Europe GmbH is the manufacturer of the Impella LP2.5 device and provided support for the ISAR-SHOCK trial.

Acute Myocardial Infarction (AMI)
Acute myocardial infarction is a medical condition characterized by a sudden blockage of blood flow to the heart muscle, causing cardiogenic shock in the study patients.<SEP>Acute Myocardial Infarction (AMI) is a medical condition

Hemodynamic Support
Hemodynamic support refers to the use of devices like Impella LP2.5 and IABP to improve blood flow

Cardiogenic Shock (CS)
Cardiogenic shock is a condition of inadequate blood flow due to heart dysfunction, which the Impella LP2.5 and IABP are used to treat.

Cardiac Index (CI)
Cardiac Index (CI) is a hemodynamic parameter defined as the primary end point of the study, measuring change from baseline after device implantation.

Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP)
Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP) is a hemodynamic parameter used to calculate Cardiac Power Index (CPI), with changes observed in both Impella and IABP groups.

Cardiac Power Index (CPI)
Cardiac Power Index (CPI) is a calculated hemodynamic parameter derived from CI and MAP, used to assess cardiac function.<SEP>Cardiac Power Index is a hemodynamic parameter measured over time after device implantation in the clinical trial.

Intra-Aortic Balloon Pump
Intra-Aortic Balloon Pump (IABP) is a medical device used as a control treatment in a clinical trial, compared against Impella.

Multiple Organ Dysfunction Score
Multiple Organ Dysfunction Score (MODS) is a scoring system used to assess organ dysfunction severity, measured at baseline and 30 days.

Hemolysis
Hemolysis is the breakdown of red blood cells, assessed by free hemoglobin levels, which were significantly higher in Impella patients.<SEP>Hemolysis is a potential adverse event or complication associated with Impella use.

Sepsis-Related Organ Failure Assessment
Sepsis-Related Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) is a scoring system used to assess organ failure, measured at baseline and 30 days.

Mortality
Mortality refers to death as an outcome, assessed via 30-day survival curves in the clinical trial.

Jordan
Jordan shares a commitment to discovery and has a significant interaction with Taylor regarding a device.<SEP>Jordan shares a commitment to discovery and has a significant interaction with Taylor regarding a device.

Alex
Alex is a character who experiences frustration and is observant of the dynamics among other characters.<SEP>Alex is a character who experiences frustration and is observant of the dynamics among other characters.

Taylor
Taylor is portrayed with authoritarian certainty and shows a moment of reverence towards a device, indicating a change in perspective.<SEP>Taylor is portrayed with authoritarian certainty and shows a moment of reverence towards a device, indicating a change in perspective.

Cruz
Cruz is associated with a vision of control and order, influencing the dynamics among other characters.<SEP>Cruz is associated with a vision of control and order, influencing the dynamics among other characters.<SEP>Cruz is associated with a vision of control and order, influencing the dynamics among other characters.

The Device
The Device is central to the story, with potential game-changing implications, and is revered by Taylor.<SEP>The Device is a technological artifact central to the story, with potential game-changing implications, and is revered by Taylor.<SEP>The Device is central to the story, with potential game-changing implications, and is revered by Taylor.<SEP>The Device is a piece of technology central to the story, with potential game-changing implications, and is revered by Taylor.<SEP>The Device is central to the story, with potential game-changing implications, and is revered by Taylor.

data.entity_name: A Randomized Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of a Percutaneous Left Ventricular Assist Device Versus Intra-Aortic Balloon Pumping for Treatment of Cardiogenic Shock Caused by Myocardial Infarction
data.content:
A Randomized Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of a Percutaneous Left Ventricular Assist Device Versus Intra-Aortic Balloon Pumping for Treatment of Cardiogenic Shock Caused by Myocardial Infarction
A clinical trial comparing the safety and efficacy of a percutaneous left ventricular assist device (Impella LP2.5) versus intra-aortic balloon pumping for treating cardiogenic shock caused by myocardial infarction.

Quarterly Earnings
Nexon Technologies reported lower-than-expected quarterly earnings, leading to a stock decline.

Rising Oil Prices
Rising oil prices, driven by supply constraints and strong demand, benefited Omega Energy's stock.<SEP>Rising oil prices are a market condition that positively impacted Omega Energy's stock and crude oil futures.<SEP>Rising oil prices are a market condition that benefits energy companies like Omega Energy.

Supply Constraints
Supply constraints are a factor supporting the rally in crude oil prices.<SEP>Supply constraints are a factor supporting the rally in crude oil prices.

Strong Demand

Safe-haven assets like gold are sought by investors during market downturns.

Investor confidence is expected to be influenced by the Federal Reserve's upcoming policy announcement.<SEP>Investor confidence is a market sentiment factor expected to be influenced by the Federal Reserve's policy announcement.

Market Stability
Market stability is expected to be influenced by the Federal Reserve's upcoming policy announcement.<SEP>Market stability is a condition expected to be influenced by the Federal Reserve's policy announcement.

Propionic Acidemia (PA)
Propionic Acidemia (PA) is a medical condition studied in the referenced research, involving chronic kidney disease (CKD) manifestations.<SEP>Propionic Acidemia is a metabolic disorder characterized by minimal hepatic capacity to metabolize propionic acid, requiring renal excretion to clear toxic metabolites.<SEP>Propionic Acidemia is a metabolic disorder studied in the context of chronic kidney disease, affecting whole-body creatine synthesis and renal function.

Cystatin C
Cystatin C is a biomarker used to estimate glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) and assess kidney function, measured in the study.

Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD)
Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a medical condition observed in PA patients, as described in the research.<SEP>Chronic Kidney Disease is a condition involving progressive loss of kidney function, with surveillance parameters including cystatin C, plasma uric acid, and 24-hour protein excretion.<SEP>Chronic Kidney Disease is a long-term complication observed in PA patients, defined by eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m2.

Creatinine
Creatinine is a serum biomarker used to estimate GFR, but its levels in PA patients are influenced by diet, sarcopenia, and impaired synthesis.<SEP>Creatinine is a biomarker for kidney function, but its levels can be affected by factors in organic acidemias and renal tubular secretion.

Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR)
Glomerular Filtration Rate is a measure of kidney function, estimated using cystatin C and creatinine, but not directly measured in this study.

Tubulointerstitial Pathology
Tubulointerstitial Pathology refers to damage to the tubules and interstitium of the kidney, observed in a case report related to PA.

Glomerular Sclerosis
Glomerular Sclerosis is a pathological condition involving scarring of the glomeruli in the kidney.

Lipocalin-2
Lipocalin-2 is a plasma biomarker associated with tubular injury, linked to serum creatinine and cystatin C levels.

Cystatin C eGFR
Cystatin C eGFR is an estimate of kidney function based on cystatin C levels, used in propionic acidemia patients.<SEP>Cystatin C eGFR is a measure of kidney function based on cystatin C levels, used to estimate GFR in PA patients.

Serum Uric Acid
Serum uric acid is a factor associated with CKD that rises with decreasing cystatin C eGFR in PA patients.

Plasma Lipocalin 2
Plasma lipocalin 2 is a biomarker associated with tubular injury in the etiology of CKD in PA.

Whole-Body Creatine Synthesis
Whole-body creatine synthesis is a metabolic process that is reduced in PA patients due to dietary and metabolic factors.

Sarcopenia
Sarcopenia is a condition of muscle wasting that contributes to lower serum creatinine levels in PA patients.

Genetics InMedicine
Genetics InMedicine is a journal or publication title repeatedly mentioned in the text, associated with brief communications.

BriEF COMMUNICATION
BriEF COMMUNICATION is a repeated phrase in the text, likely referring to a type of article or section in the Genetics InMedicine journal.

National Institutes of Health (NIH)
The National Institutes of Health (NIH) is a research organization that reviewed and approved the study protocol and provided clinical data.

NIH Clinical Research Center
The NIH Clinical Research Center is a facility where patients with PA were evaluated.

ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT02890342
ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT02890342 is a unique identifier for a natural history study on PA.

PCCB
PCCB is a gene associated with propionic acidemia, with various mutations listed in the input text.

PCCA
PCCA is a gene associated with propionic acidemia, with mutations listed in the input text.

c.1259_1260insT p.Glu421Ter
c.1259_1260insT p.Glu421Ter is a specific mutation in the PCCB gene resulting in a premature stop codon.

c.1225_1227delATC p.Ile409del
c.1225_1227delATC p.Ile409del is a specific deletion mutation in the PCCB gene.

c.337C>T p.Arg113Ter
c.337C>T p.Arg113Ter is a specific mutation in the PCCB gene resulting in a premature stop codon.

c.386_387delTinsAAC p.Phe129Ter
c.386_387delTinsAAC p.Phe129Ter is a complex mutation in the PCCB gene resulting in a premature stop codon.

c.1552delG p.Asp538Thrfs*33
c.1552delG p.Asp538Thrfs*33 is a deletion mutation in the PCCB gene causing a frameshift.

Creatinine eGFR
Creatinine eGFR is an estimate of kidney function based on serum creatinine levels, used in propionic acidemia patients.

eGFR
eGFR (estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate) is a measure of kidney function, with values classified as <90 mL/min/1.73 m2 indicating reduced function.

data.entity_name: National Institutes of Health (NIH) Propionic Acidemia Cohort
data.content:
National Institutes of Health (NIH) Propionic Acidemia Cohort
The NIH Propionic Acidemia Cohort is a study group of patients with propionic acidemia, providing demographics, genetics, and renal-related findings.

Fisher's Exact Test
Fisher's exact test is a statistical method used to analyze the classification of eGFR values, yielding a P value <0.0001.

Propionic Acidemia (PA) Patients
Propionic acidemia patients are individuals with a genetic metabolic disorder, studied for renal function in the NIH cohort.

Nontransplanted Patients
Nontransplanted patients are propionic acidemia patients who have not undergone liver transplantation, analyzed for eGFR trends.

Regression Lines
Regression lines compare creatinine eGFR and cystatin C eGFR in nontransplanted patients, showing differences in intercepts and slopes.

Intact Parathyroid Hormone (PTH)
Intact parathyroid hormone (PTH) is a laboratory parameter measured in the study, with elevated levels found in 3 out of 31 participants.

1,25-OH-Vitamin D
1,25-OH-Vitamin D is a laboratory parameter measured in the study, with low levels found in 2 out of 31 participants.

Erythropoietin
Erythropoietin is a laboratory parameter measured in the study, with elevated levels found in 7 out of 27 subjects, correlating with bone marrow suppression.

24-Hour Creatinine Clearance
24-hour creatinine clearance is a laboratory parameter used to assess renal function in the study.

Abdominal Ultrasound
Abdominal ultrasound is an imaging technique used in the study to examine renal anatomy.

Transthoracic Echocardiography
Transthoracic echocardiography is an imaging technique used in the study to assess cardiac function.

Scriver CR
Scriver CR is an editor of the book "The metabolic basis of inherited diseases".

Horwich AL
Horwich AL is a co-author of the work "Urea cycle enzymes" published in "The metabolic basis of inherited diseases".

data.entity_name: The Metabolic Basis of Inherited Diseases
data.content:
The Metabolic Basis of Inherited Diseases
The Metabolic Basis of Inherited Diseases is a book, 6th edition, published by McGraw-Hill in 1989, containing the chapter "Urea cycle enzymes".

Beaudet AL
Beaudet AL is an editor of "The metabolic basis of inherited diseases" and a co-author of works on argininosuccinate synthetase and gene transfer.

Valle D
Valle D is an editor of the book "The metabolic basis of inherited diseases".

Bachmann C
Bachmann C is an author of the article on N-acetylglutamate synthetase deficiency published in the New England Journal of Medicine.

Sly WS
Sly WS is an editor of the book "The metabolic basis of inherited diseases".

Ilow SW
Ilow SW is an author of the work "Urea cycle enzymes" published in "The metabolic basis of inherited diseases".

Krahenbuhl S
Krahenbuhl S is a co-author of

Liver Transplantation
Liver transplantation is a surgical procedure used to correct metabolic consequences of urea cycle deficiencies, as established by cumulative experience in multiple cases.

Human ASS
Human ASS (argininosuccinate synthetase) is a gene that has been successfully transferred via virus-mediated methods in rodents, suggesting potential therapeutic applications.

Urea Cycle Deficiencies
Urea cycle deficiencies are metabolic disorders for which liver transplantation has been shown to be effective, correcting metabolic consequences beyond alternative therapies.

Enzyme Replacement Therapy
Enzyme replacement therapy is a potential future treatment approach for urea cycle deficiencies, envisioned as a step beyond whole-liver transplantation.

Virus-Mediated Gene Transfer
Virus-mediated gene transfer is a technique involving the introduction of functional complementary DNA into cells, accomplished in rodents for human ASS.

Hepatocyte Transplantation
Hepatocyte transplantation is a cell-based therapy considered as an alternative to whole-liver transplantation for treating metabolic diseases.

Dietary Protein Restriction
Dietary protein restriction is a conventional treatment for urea cycle deficiencies, but is less effective than liver transplantation in correcting metabolic consequences.

data.entity_name: Alternative Pathways of Waste Nitrogen Synthesis
data.content:
Alternative Pathways of Waste Nitrogen Synthesis
Alternative pathways of waste nitrogen synthesis and excretion are therapeutic approaches that are less effective than liver transplantation for correcting metabolic consequences.

Standard Liver Function Abnormalities
Standard liver function abnormalities are deviations in typical liver function tests, corrected by orthotopic liver transplantation.

Orthotopic Liver Transplantation
Orthotopic liver transplantation is a surgical procedure that replaces a diseased liver with a healthy donor liver, used to correct metabolic abnormalities.

Plasma Amino Acid Abnormalities
Plasma amino acid abnormalities refer to deviations from normal levels of amino acids in the blood, corrected by orthotopic liver transplantation.

Hyperammonemia
Hyperammonemia is a metabolic condition characterized by elevated ammonia levels in the blood, corrected by liver transplantation in the patients described.

14-Day-Old Boy
A 14-day-old boy with carbamyl phosphate synthetase-I deficiency who underwent orthotopic liver transplantation.

Carbamyl Phosphate Synthetase-I Deficiency
Carbamyl phosphate synthetase-I deficiency is a urea cycle enzyme deficiency causing hyperammonemia and metabolic abnormalities.

35-Year-Old Man
A 35-year-old man with argininosuccinic acid synthetase deficiency who underwent orthotopic liver transplantation.

Argininosuccinic Acid Synthetase Deficiency
Argininosuccinic acid synthetase deficiency is a urea cycle enzyme deficiency causing hyperammonemia and metabolic abnormalities.

Patient 2
Patient 2 is a subject whose plasma amino acids before and after transplantation are summarized in Table 2. Preoperative high citrulline and modest arginine elevations were corrected postoperatively, but ornithine levels were slightly elevated at 3 and 20 months. The native liver was enlarged with cholestasis, massive steatosis, and hemosiderosis, with periportal fibrosis but no cirrhosis. The liver homogenate was devoid of ASS activity but had significant CPS-1, OTC, and AS activity.<SEP>Patient 2 is a subject whose urea cycle enzyme activities and plasma amino acid levels were analyzed before and after liver transplantation.

Patient 1
Patient 1 is a subject who underwent surgery and had normal plasma amino acid levels 34 months post-surgery, with detectable citrulline levels. The native liver showed minor nonspecific abnormalities on light microscopy and normal electron microscopy except for nonspecific mitochondrial elongation. CPS-I activity was undetectable in the liver homogenate.

Arginine
Arginine is an amino acid that showed modest elevations preoperatively in Patient 2, which were corrected at all measured postoperative times.

Citrulline
Citrulline is an amino acid that became detectable in Patient 1 after surgery and was present at extremely high preoperative levels in Patient 2, which were corrected postoperatively.

Ornithine
Ornithine is an amino acid with a normal range of 40 to 100 μmol/L. In Patient 2, it was slightly elevated at 3 months (153 μmol/L) and 20 months (124 μmol/L) postoperatively.

Urea Cycle Enzymes
Urea cycle enzymes include CPS-I, OTC, ASS, and AS, which were analyzed in liver tissues from Patient 2.

CPS-I
CPS-I is a urea cycle enzyme whose activity was measured in the liver tissue of Patient 2.

OTC
OTC is a urea cycle enzyme whose activity was measured in the liver tissue of Patient 2.

ASS
ASS is a urea cycle enzyme whose activity was measured in the liver tissue of Patient 2.

AS
AS is a urea cycle enzyme whose activity was measured in the liver tissue of Patient 2.

Kagoshima
Kagoshima is a city in Japan where the Department of Biochemistry of Kagoshima University is located.

data.content:
Department of Biochemistry of Kagoshima University
The Department of Biochemistry of Kagoshima University is the institution where the liver tissues from Patient 2 were analyzed for urea cycle enzyme activities.

Japan
Japan is the country where the analysis of liver tissues from Patient 2 was conducted.

Case 1
A medical case report describing a patient who underwent surgery and immunosuppression, experienced severe rejection, and developed an Aspergillus brain abscess.

The Federal Reserve
The Federal Reserve is the central bank of the United States whose upcoming policy announcement is expected to influence investor confidence and market stability.

todo1992
A literature reference or citation identifier for a medical publication from 1992.

Surgery
A medical procedure performed on the patient, likely an organ transplant (OLT).

Immunosuppression
A medical treatment involving cyclosporine and prednisone to prevent organ rejection.

Cyclosporine
An immunosuppressive drug used in the patient's treatment.

Prednisone
A corticosteroid drug used in the patient's immunosuppression regimen.

Severe Rejection
A serious medical complication beginning in postoperative week 3, requiring augmented steroid dosage and OKT3.

OKT3
A monoclonal antibody drug added to treat severe rejection.

Aspergillus Brain Abscess
A fungal infection in the brain that was successfully treated with antibiotics, open drainage, and a ventriculoperitoneal shunt.

Bacteremia
A bloodstream infection

Omega Energy
Rising oil prices contributed to Omega Energy's stock gain.

Nexon Technologies
Nexon Technologies' stock plummeted after reporting lower-than-expected quarterly earnings.

Crude Oil
Omega Energy's stock gain is driven by rising crude oil prices, reflecting a positive correlation between the company and the commodity.
